claim_family: Microsoft and OpenAI formed an exclusive computing partnership in 2019 focused on Azure AI supercomputing technologies.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not infer that commercialization succeeded or that OpenAI became dependent in all respects.

claim_family: Microsoft described an Azure-hosted AI supercomputer built for OpenAI in 2020.
allowed_in_chapter: Use as infrastructure relationship and cloud-capacity evidence.

claim_family: Microsoft exclusively licensed GPT-3 in 2020 while OpenAI continued offering models through its API.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for primitive-to-platform route and distribution strategy.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Do not overstate ownership or future model rights; OpenAI-side page is a capture gap.

claim_family: GitHub Copilot launched as a technical preview in VS Code, developed with OpenAI and powered by Codex.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for cursor-level LLM product surface.
blocked_or_needs_followup: No productivity, adoption, legal, correctness, or replacement claims.

claim_family: Azure OpenAI Service reached general availability in January 2023 and framed model access with enterprise/cloud benefits.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for enterprise access route and cloud packaging.
blocked_or_needs_followup: No customer outcome, revenue, paid-seat, margin, or adoption claims.

claim_family: Microsoft and OpenAI extended their partnership in January 2023 after ChatGPT made the model interface publicly legible.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for chronology and strategic platform framing.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Keep investment, deployment, and category claims attributed to Microsoft.

claim_family: ChatGPT was made available in Azure OpenAI Service in March 2023.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for cloud-service conversion of the ChatGPT product.
blocked_or_needs_followup: No usage volume or enterprise uptake claims.

claim_family: Microsoft 365 Copilot was introduced as a work assistant across Microsoft 365 apps and tied to Microsoft Graph/workplace context.
allowed_in_chapter: Use for work-surface distribution and product positioning.
blocked_or_needs_followup: No productivity gains, customer ROI, or revenue claims.

claim_family: Microsoft/OpenAI partnership revenue, cloud margin, compute cost, or profit-sharing economics.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Needs filings, reliable reporting, or row-normalized financial evidence.

claim_family: GitHub Copilot or Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption, productivity, replacement, or enterprise outcome claims.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Needs customer-side studies, filings, or independently normalized metrics.

claim_family: Azure OpenAI Service customer counts, workload volumes, model-serving economics, or active-use figures.
allowed_in_chapter: Keep as access-route and product packaging claims.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Needs source-specific customer/usage evidence and metric definitions.

claim_family: Bing/search share, ad revenue, or consumer-behavior effects from OpenAI integration.
allowed_in_chapter: Do not quantify in this pass.
blocked_or_needs_followup: Needs search/product chapter evidence and dated market metrics.
